top_signals.title: Qualcomm Says Data Center Chips to Produce ‘Billions’ in ’27
top_signals.summary: Bloomberg's Mandeep Singh previews Micron earnings and the larger tech environment as companies at the forefront of AI forecast massive earnings. Singh said he finds similarities between the hold oil companies have on energy prices with how chip companies have a control of AI memory and are unlikely to increase that capacity as they bring in money.   (Source: Bloomberg)
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Micron and Qualcomm Lift Chipmakers Amid Rapid AI Growth
top_signals.summary: Shares of semiconductor stocks are soaring in extended trading on Wednesday, following a strong financial report from Micron Technology Inc. that underscored how artificial intelligence remains a major vector for growth.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Ships Begin Sailing Through Hormuz According to UN Evacuation Plan
top_signals.summary: Ships have begun sailing through the Strait of Hormuz under a new scheme by the U.N.'s shipping agency to evacuate vessels trapped there by the conflict, a spokesperson said on Wednesday.The initiative, which has taken months to conclude, will enable hundreds of ships with some 11…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Moscow Refinery May Stay Offline Until 2027
top_signals.summary: The Moscow Oil Refinery is highly unlikely to resume production before 2027 after suffering extensive structural damage from multiple strikes by Ukraine’s long-range drones, dealing a further blow to a country grappling with severe fuel shortages, Reuters reports. Operated by Gazprom Neft, the facility in southeast Moscow is likely to remain offline for at least six months due to the severity of the damage it sustained after being struck twice within a single week in mid-June. According to industry sources cited by Reuters, the strikes compromised…
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Gousto puts 290 jobs at risk in warehouse closure
top_signals.summary: Recipe box maker Gousto has put 290 jobs at risk as part of its plans to close a warehouse in Lincolnshire. The company, which delivers food boxes for people to cook meals at home, said it is closing its Lincolnshire base in a bid to keep prices down in a competitive market. Gousto, a rival [...]

top_signals.title: Tank maker KNDS plans stock market listing, Germany to buy 40% stake
top_signals.summary: The firm showed a proposed battle tank at Eurosatory defense exhibition in Paris boasting a French turret and gun mounted on a German Leopard 2 chassis.

top_signals.title: BCC’s Haviland: Burnham must make growth his number one priority
top_signals.summary: Andy Burnham must place economic growth at the heart of his premiership and promise to bring down costs for businesses if he wants the Labour party to win back the trust of the private sector, the boss of Britain’s largest industry body will say. In a speech at the British Chambers of Commerce’s annual conference, [...]

top_signals.title: Mandiant reveals how Cisco SD-WAN zero-day attacks gained root access
top_signals.summary: New details have been revealed on how hackers exploited a Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN vulnerability tracked as CVE-2026-20245 in zero-day attacks to create rogue root accounts on targeted devices. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, April 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit decreased in April 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit decreased from $56.6 billion in March (revised) to $55.9 billion in April, as exports increased more than imports. The goods deficit decreased $2.4 billion in April to $83.7 billion. The services surplus decreased $1.7 billion in April to $27.8 billion. Full Text
